entry.title: inspection points time
entry.content: Hello! Tell me please, How to set an interval in days to create a inspection point. I know that in QP02 operations, but where exactly? If possible, please tell me the process of the inspection points. I figured out the creation, but I didn't fully understand the process itself

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP FIORI app Deployment to Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Hi, 

Your UI app should use the CF destination for connection to service layer.

You can then configure your CF destination to any OData provider (e.g S/4 HANA). 

https://developers.sap.com/group.abap-custom-ui-s4hana-cloud.html

 

Regards

Vishal Kapoor

entry.title: Re: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content: Provided plant, material unit and STGE_LOC but its return total stock not the actual stock lying on storage location.

entry.title: Re: Activate App support

entry.title: Re: Activate App support
entry.content:
FLP App Support is not available in S/4Hana Public Cloud.

most of the usual issues tackled by App Support should not happen in Public Cloud.

entry.title: New Pipeline Concept features: more bypass options and custom extensions
entry.content:
We have recently shipped a new version 1.0.8 of the Pipeline Concept were we introduced the following new features:

  • Option to combine receiver and interface determination in one single XSLT mapping for an improved runtime behavior
  • Option to bypass interface determination pipeline step in case of Recipient List pattern without interface split
  • Option to bypass receiver determination pipeline step in case of interface split scenarios with one single receiver only
  • Supporting header-based XSLT routing conditions if message body is non-XML
  • Extension for custom receiver determination instead of XSLT from Partner Directory
  • Extension for custom interface determination instead of XSLT from Partner Directory
  • New pipeline processing log providing more details if message is stored to the dead letter queue
  • Fixed standard retry handling for generic integration flows with splitter to avoid message duplicates

The package can be accessed from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. If you have already copied the package to your workspace in your SAP Integration Suite tenant, you can simply run the update of the package to be able to use the latest features.

Let me briefly describe some of the new features.

Further bypass options

With version 1.0.6, we have already shipped a bypass option for pure Point-to-Point scenarios, see New Pipeline Concept features: new partner id definition, alternative partner, bypass option. With the latest version, you now have the option to either bypass the receiver determination or the interface determination depending on your integration scenario. For an interface split scenario with one single receiver only, you can bypass the receiver determination. For a Recipient List pattern with one interface per receiver, you can bypass the interface determination. In any cases, by applying the different bypass options you can save at least one JMS queue and one pipeline step which leads to an improved runtime behavior.

Further runtime improvements can be achieved by combining the receiver determination xpaths and the interface determination xpaths in one single XSLT. This use case is actually a special case of the interface determination bypass option. Here, the receiver determination pipeline step has been enhanced supporting a receiver determination XSLT that contains xpaths conditions for both the receiver and the interface determination. The receiver determination pipeline step then splits both the number of receivers and the number of interfaces. This way, you only need to read, decode, and run one single XSLT instead of multiple interface determination XSLTs for each receiver.

Here's an example of such a combined XSLT:

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="3.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
  <xsl:param name="dc_country"/>
  <xsl:template match="/">
    <ns0:Receivers xmlns:ns0="http://sap.com/xi/XI/System">
      <ReceiverNotDetermined>
        <Type>Error</Type>
        <DefaultReceiver/>
      </ReceiverNotDetermined>
      <xsl:if test="/*:Item/Category = 'Keyboards'">
        <Receiver>
          <Service>Receiver_11</Service>
          <Interfaces>
            <xsl:if test="/*:Item/Quantity = '10'">
              <Interface>
                <Index>1</Index>                       
                <Service>/PIP/Step07/Samples/Scenario_1/Receiver_11/IfIdx1</Service>
              </Interface>
            </xsl:if>
            <xsl:if test="/*:Item/CurrencyCode = 'EUR'">
              <Interface>
                <Index>2</Index>
                <Service>/PIP/Step07/Samples/Scenario_1/Receiver_11/IfIdx2</Service>
              </Interface>
            </xsl:if>
            <xsl:if test="/*:Item/Quantity != '10'">
              <Interface>
                <Index>3</Index>
                <Service>/PIP/Step07/Samples/Scenario_1/Receiver_11/IfIdx3</Service>
              </Interface>
            </xsl:if>
          </Interfaces>
        </Receiver>
      </xsl:if>
      <xsl:if test="(/*:Item/Category = 'Keyboards') or (/*:Item/Category = 'Software')">
        <Receiver>
          <Service>Receiver_12</Service>
          <Interfaces>
            <xsl:if test="$dc_country = 'DE'">
              <Interface>
                <Index>1</Index>
                <Service>/PIP/Step07/Samples/Scenario_1/Receiver_12/IfIdx1</Service>
              </Interface>
            </xsl:if>
            <xsl:if test="$dc_country != 'DE'">
              <Interface>
                <Index>2</Index>
                <Service>/PIP/Step07/Samples/Scenario_1/Receiver_12/IfIdx2</Service>
              </Interface>
            </xsl:if>
          </Interfaces>
        </Receiver>
      </xsl:if>
    </ns0:Receivers>
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

 

As you can see, the XSLT contains the receiver components as well as the receiver interface index and the end points of the scenario-specific outbound processing flows where the mappings and the delivery to the receivers are done. By providing the end point information here, the interface determination pipeline step is bypassed.

Custom extensions

As an alternative to the standard receiver or interface determination based on an XSLT from the Partner Directory, you can implement your own custom receiver or interface determination. The generic integration flows support a kind of custom exit that you can use to run your own custom logic without changing the delivered generic integration flows. To do so, you must implement a separate scenario-specific integration flow that's called instead of the standard XSLT mappings.

You do not need to create the scenario-specific integration flows from scratch, the updated Pipeline package contains templates that you can copy and adapt to your needs. The templates have the target XSDs as references which eases the implementation effort. So, you can create a message mapping that maps to the target structure or alternatively you can create your own groovy scripts or use any other logic which you prefer. The target XSD for the receiver determination by the way supports the combined receiver and interface determination as described above. So, when using the custom extensions, you can also leverage the bypass options.

Non-XML payloads

When running an XSLT mapping to determine the list of receivers or interfaces for non-XML messages, you usually run into an error even if the conditions are based on headers only because the XSLT expects an XML. To overcome this error, a dummy XML is created right before running the XSLT. With this, you are able to process non-XML messages within the pipeline without the need to convert into XML before.

Processing log

At various places during message processing, information is stored in a new header which we pass across the pipeline steps. We decided to add the log information whenever we call groovy scripts to avoid to make too many changes to the integration flow model. Whenever a message is stored in the dead letter queue because the maximum of retries has been exceeded, the log information is attached to the message processing log.

Retry handling

For the generic receiver and interface determination integration flows where the message is split, transaction handling ensures that split messages are rolled back in case of an error. However, if the message is forwarded to the dead letter queue, the message processing ends with either a message end event or an escalation end event so that in rare cases already split messages might be committed. This may lead to duplicates if the message is moved from the dead letter queue to the actual queue for reprocessing. To avoid duplicates, we had to change the standard retry handling within the receiver and the interface determination flows. When the actual maximum number of retries have been exceeded, we need to do an additional retry and then check right at the beginning of the integration flows before splitting the message. For the other generic flows that do not carry out a split, the retry handling kept unchanged.

 

If you like to try out the pipeline concept in general and the new features in particular, check out this github repository where I describe how to setup sample scenarios using the pipeline concept. Here, we have added the following new scenarios and scenario variants leveraging the new features:

  • Scenario 1A, scenario 2A and scenario 2B cover the bypass options including the combined receiver and interface determination.
  • Scenario 2C and scenario 4A cover the custom extensions samples.
  • Scenario 7 covers the non-XML payload use case.

As usually, in case of feedback, feel free to reach out to me.

entry.title: Re: New 1H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
Hi @EvangeliaP 

thanks for reaching out. Creating counters for allowance types is not possible. Allowances are 'manually recorded pay types'. They are not processed by SF time valuation.

But I am curious what is your use case. Could you explain?

Thank you

Stephanie

entry.title: Functional location Enhanced search Help
entry.content: Functional location Enhanced search Help is not working, whereas for Equipment it is working

entry.title: Can't find Active Directory system under Target system of IPS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I got two CIS tenants, one with "trail" and other one as "additional tenant" and for some reason I can't find Active Directory or MS Entra ID or LDAP as Target as given in help document.

https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning/target-microsoft-active-directory

However, I can see these systems as my source, but I want these systems as my target.

Not sure if I'm missing any configuration, or is it because of the CIS tenant type I'm subscribed to?

-RP

entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have issues controling smartforms when using Submit statement.
To be precise, I can't stack smartforms in one spool and release them all together.
Did anyone had any similar problems?

entry.title: Re: SAP Document Management System (DMS) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and On Premise
entry.content: Good information..

entry.title: Understand Enterprise Management Layer (EML) using SAP Signavio
entry.content:
What is Enterprise Management Layer (EML)

The Enterprise Management Layer (EML) is a upgraded version of SAP Model Company combining all the essential SAP Best Practices, considered as a Global Template for SAP S/4HANA implementations (or) transformations for the customers located in various countries.

The EML as template offers pre-configured end-to-end business processes in all the SAP Application areas. The major focus of EML is on the complex accounting and reporting requirements based on the country (local government) level requirements, this helps to meet the global/local accounting principles.

Additionally, EML helps to bring all the customers who are having heavy customizations in their ECC & other business applications, will bring back to standard to make sure with the sustainable solution with limited maintainability.

In April 2020, SAP enhanced SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition by delivering business content and updated services as part of the subscription. SAP bundles into the technical offering of software capabilities the ability to immediately consume a pre-defined scope of pre-activated SAP Best Practices business processes virtually “out-of-the-box”.

With the offering of a pre-configured business template usable by customers and partners as a foundation to jumpstart their implementation project, SAP significantly contributes to the key cloud principles:

  • Reduce Time-to-Value
  • Reduce Total Cost of Ownership by reducing cost for implementation
  • Contribute to the speed to leverage New Innovation

The Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition (EML) allows customers to execute business processes based on SAP Best Practices. Where the standard SAP Best Practices typically focus on national companies running operations within one country only, the EML focuses on international customers that run subsidiaries within multiple countries around the globe.

 

Business Applications and Process Standardization

The appliance “Enterprise Management Layer as available in S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition” consist out of 2 virtual machines.

  1. S/4HANA with Required DB Application
  2. Adobe Document Services (ADS)

The "Enterprise Management Layer, as available in S/4HANA Cloud” solution can be delivered via Cloud Appliance Library any of the Cloud Accounts.

 

 

The Enterprise Management Layer is a global template which can be tailored to each customer’s requirements in 2 simple stages:

  1. Initial build: Each customer specifies the initial scope and certain parameters for the build of their system such as the countries in scope, but also the customer’s group currency and fiscal year variant. For each country, around 160 standard scope items(Approx) will be available. In addition, scope options which might require additional software licenses can be selected for functionality.
  2. Customer project: Fit-to-Standard workshops are recommended to maximize re-use of SAP delivered ready-to-run business processes while carefully assessing where a customer’s business requires tailoring or extending the solution.

SAP Model Company Vs Best Practices Vs EML

An SAP Model Company is planned & designed for only one country. This will enable us to create a example company code structure which will help us to get the business idea for the best use of SAP Products & Processes.

SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides ready-to-use business process content for core business processes in R2R, S2P, P2P, OTC etc. Typically, SAP Best Practices are more Industry oriented standard processes. SAP Best Practices are prepared more towards the Industry Solutions developed by SAP as part of ECC Infrastructure.

By combining the above two experiences from Model Company and Best Practices, SAP converted and combined all together and evaluated the Enterprise Management Layer.

EML is the solution template which provides the complete value chain as per the multiple industry standards across multiple countries. Technically, EML will help to design the clean core standards (Low Code/No Code) across the various process chains. This will help to keep the system clean, and clear to keep in automated support models, ultimately reduce huge cost for enhancements, maintenance and post live support.

Speed, Content, Quality & Flexibility

Today’s digital transformation is driving rapid change across all industries, and its effects are profound. We are witnessing a paradigm shift with the commoditization of cloud computing and how its openness, flexibility, and scalability is driving digitalization globally. With billions of connected devices and the copious amount of data they generate, we see an ever-increasing demand to store and process distributed data in real-time.

Deliver the Quality Content with Flexible Options with in planned Speed – Activation of the Best SAP Practices (in the form of EML Process) for 43 countries.

Once the Technical setup up of the SAP S/4HANA system as per the Administration Guide provided, the complete clean core solution is available in 25+ languages, creating clients, enabling Fiori apps, creating users, activating business functions, etc., will be the piece of walk to run the system with Basic and standard system landscape.

As the EML template exposed with SAP Landscape, complete quality content will be available and where the business process discussions sessions will help for alignment. Taylor the country coverage and the functional scope of your customer-specific enterprise management layer for optimal fit. Use the template as a jump start for your SAP S/4HANA implementation and deploy it throughout your system landscape, including in your Development, Quality and Production systems.

Adaption of EML for S/4 Transformation

SAP Signavio, the Product to speak the EML with the Business, that will help to Initiate, design, analyze and improve the Business

Below are the various kinds of modules separated in Signavio, which will help to design the Business process at various steps. Below process image helps us to understand the details and importance of EML enabled SAP Signavio as part of Solution design and development.

 

 

Analyze & Monitor

SAP Signavio Process Insights – helps customers in getting an overview of the overall performance using End to End process flows, Process performance indicators (PPI), Innovation Recommendations & Correction Recommendations (Best Practice). This will help Customer can identify which business areas or processes needs to be prioritized for improvements.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – process data from both SAP and non-SAP systems, SAP Signavio process Intelligence with the help of its powerful process mining capabilities can identify want needs to be improved based on the raw data. SAP Signavio process intelligence helps by providing visibility into the real process execution which provides insights into the previously unknown process and experience related inefficiencies. This helps Customer to Identify Process/Experience related inefficiencies & identify What improvements are required.

Design & Simulate

SAP Signavio Process Manager – help the customer get an inside-Out view of the business processes, helps the customer to design (make improvements/Changes to existing process) and analyze the processes, the solution also provides the customer the flexibility to compare and simulate the end-to-end flow with the changes, to see what the impacts are and to see how the new process works based on real scenarios before implementing the changes.

SAP Signavio Journey Modeler – helps the customer to improve the customer experience by providing the tools which allows to design appealing customer Journeys (How the final user experience the operations -Look and feel), Once designed the customer can connect these customer journeys to the business processes & systems.

Rollout & Improve

Once the design is complete the next step is to roll out the solution to the organization, SAP Signavio Process Governance provides the customer with the ability to manage and govern the rollout of the solution (Process and customer Experience). SAP Signavio Process governance provides the customer with the ability to adapt solution quickly and effectively.

On top of all the above functions SAP Process Automation provides the capability to reduce the manual and repetitive tasks with the help of iRPA (intelligent Robotic Process Automation), Workflow & Low code-No Code functions.

Conclusion

With the changing world with complex processes involved, Business need the full and transparent insights. It helps the organizations to adapt fast, and know-how about the upgraded systems / applications / processes in the business models. So, SAP Signavio offers business process management that literally every business needs, coupled with industry-next practices and process mining.

entry.title: Re: Compensation - Update compensation forms
entry.content:
Hello @xavierlegarrec 

We have already scheduled this daily job but it is not working properly in case of future hires
The employees employee history is getting pulled but they are not present in the form

Best Regards
Ravi

entry.title: Re: Error-assigning a company code to a controlling area
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please use transaction OKKP. Which is standard process for assignment of Company code to Controling area.

Regards,

Manjunath Rao.

entry.title: Attachments on Remittance or Payment status information
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to send Check Copies on Remittance status information from Non-SAP ERP System to Ariba Invoice Management system. Is anyone aware that we can send attachments over the Remittance status/Payment status file from Non-SAP ERP to Ariba Network/Ariba Invoice Management solution?

If so, could you please share your experience or any guidance around this?

Please note that, since this is with Non-SAP system, we don't have CIG and we have SAP BTP in scope.

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Re: SAP Central Finance & using AIF effectively
entry.content:
Hi Nitin

In this very interesting blog, at a certain point, you write

"So if you are sending 800 messages one by one then you are asking AIF to create 800*2 =1600 background jobs"

You finish off by writing "This issue can be avoided by doing this set up correctly and using other options to execute AIF RUN."

Can you please detail some more what options you are talking about?

Thanks again

entry.title: Re: Is Hungarian Notation as bad as "everyone" says it is? They are wrong!!! (oops clickba
entry.content: There are PROs and CONs for both sides and at the end some people in the company decide the direction and take all points into account. The points 4-6 can be avoided with a good configured ATC. It checks the scope and naming, so everybody in the system uses the same names and no one asks again, when the transport release is not working. To get a Clean ABAP naming without static code checks will be much harder.

entry.title: Re: New 1H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
Hi @StephanieCR thanks for your prompt reply! Basically, there is a requirement with a customer who works with factory workers in Italy, they need to record their Canteen Breaks every day. We decided to cover this requirement with the simplest way possible, instead of using Time Events, we would just record a unit of Canteen Break for every day this is used (this would flow directly into SF from their Portals) and send this total number of days per month back to payroll system for payment processing. Therefore, ideally i would need to have a monthly counter to send the total number of "events" per month, rather than send it on a daily break down. 

I guess i can always get this information using a report, if I also need to crosscheck the amount of money the employee got deducted against the number of times they had used the Canteen over a monthly period.

Thanks,

BR,

Evangelia

entry.title: AFC - Client requirement: Hierarchy based on task list folder
entry.content:
Our client has recently acquired AFC and we are working to establish a hierarchy that aligns with their requirements. The client prefers to create the hierarchy based on task list levels rather than company code levels. Their goal is to prevent task duplication during the month-end close, as one person is responsible for performing the same tasks across all company codes. Therefore, they would like to build a hierarchy based on task folders and then assign company codes to specific tasks. Currently AFC doesnt allow to assign company codes to tasks.

Does anyone have a solution for this?

entry.title: Create a Launcher fails
entry.content:
Hi, I cannot add a Launcher to my Project in section "Launch Manually from the agent". Error message is "Yo can´t use this automation because it contains data type inputs". My problem is that when this project was used in the old irpa version, this did not seem to be an issue. How can I fix this problem?

Thanks, Katja

entry.title: Query with SAP Note 3508244 - Incompatible Change in Data Lake SQL on Files Behavior in QRC 4/2024
entry.content:
As the Data lake Relational Engine native SQL on Files will be removed in QRC 4/2024, I was checking the SAP Note 3508244 - Incompatible Change in Data Lake SQL on Files Behavior in QRC 4/2024 

According to this Note, I created a Remote Source REMOTE_SOF and Virtual Table in SAP HANA Database using the below command 

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE NATION(N_NATIONKEY INT, N_NAME VARCHAR(25), N_REGIONKEY INT, N_COMMENT VARCHAR(152)) AT REMOTE_SOF."/nation.csv" AS CSV FIELD DELIMITED BY ',';

The table was created successfully. However, when I try to execute the below select query

SELECT * FROM NATION;

The last entry in the nation.csv file is not getting displayed in the query output. Has anyone encountered a similar issue? If so, could anyone please provide guidance or kindly suggest a resolution?"

entry.title: Integration Suit with OpenConnector Google drive integration
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 
i am following this article to integrate Google drive with integration suit

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/openconnectors-googledrive-integration-made-sim...

At the end when i trying to send request through postman it give 401 Unauthorized error.

 

 

So for that i am providing SAP Process Integration Runtime Instance Client id and secret key as basic authentication.
After that it give 500 Internal server error.

 

Can anyone help me out for that?

 

@Sriprasadsbhat

entry.title: Re: Cannot create a single file installer in SAP Installa...
entry.content: Jude, but according to SAP Note #3525923 the 32-bit version of package in not compatible with SAP GUI 8.00 64-bit, that we are planning to install.

entry.title: Re: Bank details not updating from successfactors to sap HCM
entry.content: Hello, did you  resolve this issue and how? thanks

entry.title: EWM LOSC - create WT for Nested HU
entry.content:
Hi,

I setup following Inbound process (from Vendor):

POSC / LOSC combination of movements with couple st. types

On the begining of process Im doing manual nesting GRed HUs into MHU (via RF Manual packing, so GRed HUs are nested under Master HU). Then Im using POSC/LOSC to move MHU (RF app Putaway by HU) from one to second to third storage type. RF application Putaway by HU works correctly for MHU including last step putaway into final bin (still MHU). But I would like to have last step (putaway into final bin) based on nested HU (original PGRed HU only). 

I read some articles about that and I found possible solution in settings for WPT / st. type:

So I tried with this settings but still I was not able to putaway nested HU, only MHU works fine. Propably I have wrong customization as there are lot of movements and not sure where to apply this settings. Also possibility this is wrong settings at all and it may not work with LOSC / POSC so my backup solution is to use Badi to unpack nested HU once they arrive into pre-final zone. 

My second question is for the WT creation - In my process Im using the WT created via RF:

Any chance how to use the same for LOSC / POSC so WT will be created during the HU scanning? Already tried this settings but only first WT is created during after HU scan but next LOSCs WTs are created automatically during the previous WT confirmation

 

Thank you

 

Standa

entry.title: Re: New 1H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
@EvangeliaP Hmmm, but don´t you need this in a daily breakdown? What happens if the employee gets an salary increasement in the middle of the month? As a sum payroll cannot properly process this. 

But currently there is no other way. Allowances cannot be processed at all in the time valuation and are currently not planned. 

You could instead of an allowance use an extra timetype- employees need to record 1:00 hour - but that´s probably as usable as an allowance unit ;-). A validation rule ensures that only 1:00 can be entered. You could sum this up in a time container per  month and replicate it then to payroll. 

Up to you.....

Best regards 

Volker

entry.title: Re: Can't find Active Directory system under Target system of IPS
entry.content: Its trail account which are using.. limited features you should expect.

entry.title: Re: Cannot create a single file installer in SAP Installa...
entry.content: I believe it's a limitation on the size of the files added to the installation server, which might need to be looked at by our development team. It is under investigation already.

entry.title: Re: Focused Build Customer Extension Field Changes to Metadata Configuration and Process Type View,
entry.content:
Hi @Markus_Scheuber,

It's good that now you can see custom fields in gateway client. If you have cleared the browser cache properly then I don't see any reason why you should not be able to see the custom fields in UI unless the visibility is marked as false in the customizing. Are the fields visible in the filter bar section of Mass Change Operations app? If you still face the issue then you can create an incident for us and I will check the issue in your prod system.

Thanks,

Imran

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner – Address Management at Business User
entry.content:
I've been having some issues with the business users.

We have the Integration with Successfactor and we also have the CUA.
The Table TBZ_V_EEWA_SRC is set up as for standard picture but some employees are not being fully updated and give errors
Error in the BP creation are these:

Employee XXXXYZ: error in updating user with workplace address.
Message No. /SHCM/EMPLOYEE_BL019

Person could not be deleted as it is still used.
Message No. AM049


any idea what it could be?

entry.title: How to add a Co-Author to your blog post?
entry.content:
It is possible to add another SAP community member as a Co-Author to your blog post. 

This functionality comes handy e.g. for SAP employees that start to work on other technical topics within SAP but might also be of interest for others as well, e.g. when you want that somebody maintains your blog post because you are on vacation.  

To do so you simply start to edit your blog post by selecting "Edit Article" from the drop down list.   

  

Then you have to scroll down and you will find on the right hand side on the bottom of the page the "Co-Authors" box.  

The tricky thing is now to find the correct username your colleague might have in the SAP community but you can get this from simply copying the same from other blog posts he or she has posted.  

Here I have taken my colleague @CarineTchoutouo as an example.  

As a result Carine now shows up as a Co-Author and would be able to change my post if I am on vacation.

entry.title: Re: Unable to fetch values from expand entity in MDK - SA...
entry.content: We have regenerated and uploaded the offline initialization, and it also includes the custom entities referenced by the navigation property, but we still cannot see data in the application.

entry.title: Re: Convert PowerApp and Power Automate Flow to SAP Build?
entry.content: Hi - you wouldn't "surface" a Flow anywhere .. they are just background processes running and/or being called from the PowerApp.  As for surfacing the PowerApp in Work Zone, you could get the direct URL of the App, and use that in a Web Content card in Work Zone (which is effectively an iFrame control that surface a specified URL).

entry.title: Help with reporting layout in SAP Datasphere/ Analytics Cloud/ Web Intelligence
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using an existing model from SAP datasphere and consuming it in SAP Analytics cloud. In Datasphere I have created several Restricted measures. 

The Raw data which I am working on is as follows:

Below is the list of all Dimensions and Measures used in the model.

Below is the list of all Restricted Measures used in the model.

In the above attachment, the measures indicated with a Red Dot represent Restricted Measures in SAP Datasphere. 

The Expressions used in each of the above Restricted Measures are as follows:

  • 2023 Full: Year = '2023'
  • 2023 Nov: Year = '2023' AND Month = '11'
  • 2024 Full: Year = '2024'
  • 2024-Nov: Year = '2024' AND Month = '11'

Likewise, in SAP Analytics cloud I have also created several dimension groups to be used along with the Restricted measures which were created in SAP datasphere.

The formula used in each of the above Calculated Dimensions include the following:

  • Cost: IF([d/"DS_Structure_Test":GL].[p/GL] = ("GL3")and[d/"DS_Structure_Test":"Doc type"].[p/Doc type]=("D5"),"Cost" ,"Others")
  • Expense: IF([d/"DS_Structure_Test":GL].[p/GL] = ("GL2")and[d/"DS_Structure_Test":"Doc type"].[p/Doc type]=("D2","D4"),"Expense" ,"Others")
  • Income1: IF([d/"DS_Structure_Test":GL].[p/GL] = ("GL1")and[d/"DS_Structure_Test":"Doc type"].[p/Doc type]=("D1","D2"),"Income" ,"Others")

In a nutshell, I was trying to group several dimensions together based on a certain condition. If the condition is true, it displays the name of the calculated dimension, else it displays "Others". 

Below is the output of the Calculated Dimensions in SAP Analytics Cloud:

The goal is to exclude the rows with "Others" in the table.

I tried using filters to the above table to exclude "Others" and below is the result:

 

Below is the expected output:

 

In SAP BW, the above output is achieved using Structures (Rows-Restricted Characteristics and columns-Restricted Key Figures) in a BeX query, but now the goal is to get the output in the same format (Rows down and Columns across) as mentioned above in SAP Datasphere/ Analytics Cloud / Web Intelligence.

 

Would be great if someone has encountered the above scenario and has achieved a solution.

Thanks in advance.

Regards, 

Saifullah Sharieef

entry.title: Asset accounting entries are showing in Payment advice
entry.content:
Why Asset accounting entries are there in Payment advice line items?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Asset accounting entries are showing in Payment advice
entry.content:
Basically if there is a exchange rate difference between the Supplier invoice dated on 01.01.2024, and the Bank Debit Advice dated on 02.02.2024 because of this the difference is getting captured with the certain amount into other discounts/credits that can be noticed in the Bank Debit Advice under the Open Items (relevant supplier invoice - other discounts/credits).
Also while selecting the relevant supplier invoice in the Bank Debit Advice under the Open Items below, Other Discounts/Credits - Payment Difference Reason: 97 - Currency exchange differences will be visible. And this difference amount (..other discounts/credits) is equally distributed into number of IMATs individually and recovered from the respective assets during the payment.

This is the reason where asset entries G/L accounts are visible in the Payment advice.

entry.title: need guidance Google Drive integration With SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
hello everyone,
 i am trying to connect my GDrive with the Integration suite  and I am following this tutorial
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/openconnectors-googledrive-integration-made-simple-with-sap-cpi-sap-cp-ocn/ba-p/13408420

 I successfully connect my Drive with an open connector 

 after that, I create a package and its integration flow .
 while creating flow when we create a content manger and provide its details 

when I am providing User, Organization, and Element in the Source Value the deployment process fails.
when I  am providing only User in the Source Value the deployment process is successful but,
after getting the endpoint when I try to GET/POST in Postman it shows an error.
"500Internal Server Error"
"An internal server error occurred: The MPL ID for the failed message is: AGdQPv6Gze2tZpIGePa0E8wPJlok"

 can anyone please guide me, on how to do that perfectly?

@Sriprasadsbhat

entry.title: Re: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Dear David,

I have been trying to contact digitalskills@sap.com without success. I finished all the steps needed for the initiative and I've sent reminders as well.

Please let me know if there's anything I can do to push the process forward, I really want to certify.

 

Thanks in advance,

Farah

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Cloud - Version 2 - How to turn mandatory...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please try 'Dynamic Properties'.

Navigate to Settings >> Extensibility Admistration >> Case Service >> Case >> Dynamic Properties.

 

Regards,

Syed Muheed Basha

entry.title: Re: Controling SMARTFORMS from SUBMIT statement
entry.content: This issue is not related to SUBMIT. You may append content to an existing Spool Request if this spool request is not "final" (search "final" or "do not append print jobs" in the print dialog/print settings), provided that you don't force the generation of a New Spool Request of course (search "new spool request" in the print dialog/print settings).

entry.title: Close TU updates Actual GI Date in VL03n
entry.content:
Hello SAPeople 

I'm having the following issue when running the outbound process with EWM and TM.

When pressing Close TU, it updates the Actual GI date in the ERP side for the Outbound delivery (VL03N). Shouldn't it get updated only when I press Post GI?

Thank you all!

entry.title: Re: Need advice to make a GET authentication API work in...
entry.content:
Hello Ludovic, 

Thanks for confirmation.

entry.title: How to use API pagination in build action project_SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am working on a process where we create an Action Project and use a destination to get the cost center data from an API.

The action works fine, but there is a large amount of data available, so I am unable to get all the data. I am receiving a timeout error. We can provide inputs such as filtering for the top records, but I want to retrieve all the data. I have read about pagination in the SAP help documents, but I am a bit confused about how to implement it.

I am using this data in form creation and approval workflows for dropdown fields.

It would be greatly appreciated if anyone could help me with this.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Data Variables' filter conditions do not work
entry.content: Hello Abdulla, Thanks for reaching out to us , kindly add the screenshots for to understand better in which place, meanwhile If your mapping from data source field to field which you are mapping is different data type, it will give error. make sure all your fields which you are assigning is same as incoming data source.

entry.title: Re: Error-assigning a company code to a controlling area
entry.content: Could you convert the (ST22) dump to a text format or, at least, insert picture into the post and not as attchment

entry.title: Migration Cockpit Usage for Loading New Assets
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We are seeking clarification regarding the capabilities of the SAP Migration Cockpit, specifically in the context of asset management. Our question is whether the Migration Cockpit can be utilized to load new assets into the system, as opposed to its typical use case of migrating legacy assets from a previous system.

 

We would appreciate guidance on the following points:

  1. Can the Migration Cockpit be configured or adapted for the purpose of uploading new assets directly?
  2. Are there any specific templates, configurations, or prerequisites required for this use case?
  3. If the Migration Cockpit is not intended for this scenario, could you recommend alternative tools or best practices within SAP for efficiently handling the mass upload of new assets?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit Usage for Loading New Assets
entry.content:
Migration Cockpit is intended to upload legacy assets.

For mass upload of new assets, please consider using several BAPIs, like BAPI_FIXEDASSET_CREATE et al.

entry.title: Re: How to use API pagination in build action project_SAP...
entry.content: To use API pagination in SAP Build Process Automation, set up an API call with pagination support. Use a loop to fetch data from each page until all results are retrieved. Store and process the data, checking for a NeXT Page or similar parameter to continue fetching. Use the http Request action to handle the API calls and manage pagination efficiently.

entry.title: Expose external API to SAP DM
entry.content:
HI, 
I am trying to test a scenario where: Expose an S/4HANA private cloud API to SAP Digital Manufacturing, and use this API as external API in the production process designer. 
My progress is as below: 

  1. Create destination on SAP BTP

    Checked connection: Green without any error. 
    User is technical user created in the ERP system. 
  2. Create server via app "Manage Web Servers" in SAP DM

    After server SCN_S4DEMO created, I connected this server to DMC_Cloud server. So now server SCN_S4DEMO will be consumed by DMC_Cloud. 
  3. Create service via app "Manage Service Registry" in SAP DM
     
    Added service definition here with response as well. 
  4. Create production process with this service via app "Production process designer" in SAP DM

    But this production process kept giving the same error at step 2: 

    If i look into the error message in debug mode, it says Failed to resolve "Host" (this info is identical to the URL stated at step 1). 

Seems like SAP DM cannot speak to the backend system but it is connected when click on Check Connection in BTP. 
Note: everything is configured based on https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/business-process-extensions-developer-s-guide/api-services-restful-and-odata

Any help is appreciated! 

KR,
Sunny

entry.title: Re: Expose external API to SAP DM
entry.content: Remark: the chosen API get production order confirmation is just for test purposes.

entry.title: SAP DM - Discrete Orders - Sampling Requirements (different across process steps)
entry.content:
Hello, we are working on some discrete production with two different cases and wanted to see how the community has approached these in their sites and implementations in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

We've been exploring splitting and merging SFC's through production process to automate these activities. Our goal is to ensure sampling requirements for our data collections in the recipe are meet at the right cadence and ensuring a clean user interface (with splitting we've been worried about having hundreds of SFC's in the Work Center POD or Operation Activity POD -- process lots are something we've been exploring for this).

Would love to get advice/suggestions from the community on approaches! Thanks in advance!

Scenario A - single machine with different zones 

Step 1Check every 2500Zone 2
Step 2Check every 5000Zone 2
Step 3Check every 7500Zone 3
Step 4Varies based on qty per boxPackaging

Scenario B - three machines different zones and physical WIP between locations/processes

Step 1Check every 2500Machine 1
Step 2Check every 5000Machine 2
Step 3Check every 7500Machine 3

entry.title: Where can I learn how to set-up SAP S/4HANA Sales and configure the essential functions?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sales learning journey.

Overview 
In this Learning journey consultants and project team members will learn how to perform configuration tasks to adapt the standard functions and settings of the SAP system to the company's business needs.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, application consultants will be able to set-up SAP S/4HANA Sales and configure the essential functions.

  • Setting up the Enterprise Structures in Sales
  • Configuring Business Partner and Customer Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Sales
  • Customizing of Sales Documents: Setting up of a Sales Document Type, Item Categories and Schedule Line Categories
  • Data Flow – Setting up the Copying Control in Sales
  • Partner Functions in Sales
  • Customizing output control and text control
  • Incomplete Documents (coming soon)
  • Outline Agreements (coming soon)

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code Storyboard
entry.content:
Hello @xinye ,

I realized that the storyboard now supports the draft entity handler creation. I was able to create my draft handlers, but somehow it disappeared again. 

Could you tell me when the draft dropdown entry is going to be displayed, based on which criteria?

And we realized a bug in the storyboard if a service has a namespace. (e.g. com.company.test)

entry.title: Re: How to use API pagination in build action project_SAP...
entry.content: Hi James, Thanks for the response. I am not using HTTP requests. We have created a destination in SAP BTP and are using principal propagation authentication. Through the Action Project, we are retrieving the data from the API. My question is, how can we implement pagination in the Action Project?

entry.title: Re: Need help or advise in triggering an work flow based...
entry.content:
Hello Edukondalu, 

Thanks for reaching out to us, please find the guidance - https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4db1676c3f114f119b500bd80ccd944d/30dcfd56d50d5c38e10000000a44147b.html

Let us know, incase if you need further clarification

entry.title: Re: Error "Failed to search Email"Failed to get Store"
entry.content: Hello Sathish, Thank you for reaching out. Could you please share the search email activity configuration for further investigation?

entry.title: Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM- Webinar Summary
entry.content:
Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM- Webinar Summary

Approach for this Blog

In this blog I have documented the summary from the Virtual Webinar Series on Enable a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM.

Summary of sessions:

  • Session 1 - Maximizing Clean Core Benefits with SAP Cloud ALM- 20th November 2024
  • Session 2 - Efficiently Managing RISE with SAP Projects: A Clean Core Approach Using SAP Cloud ALM-21st November 2024
  • Session 3 - Streamlining Business Processes While Preserving a Clean Core- 26th November 2024
  • Session 4 - Ensuring a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM: Test, Change, and Deployment Management- 28th November 2024
  • Session 5 - Operational Excellence with SAP Cloud ALM: Driving the Clean Core Initiative – 3rd December 2024
  • New & Planned offerings on the Cloud ALM Roadmap for clean core adoption
  • Planned Future Roadmap to enhance Cloud ALM capability

Session 1 - Maximizing Clean Core Benefits with SAP Cloud ALM- 20th November 2024

What is Clean Core?

Clean core is a journey, which needs to be adapted by following a step-by-step approach.

Greenfield

  • Unique opportunity to follow clean core principles right from beginning.
  • Establish strong governance.

Brownfield

  • Establish clean core principle for every new deployment.
  • Plan your journey towards a clean core.

Benefits of Clean Core

Case Study of Hitachi

Challenges

  • 9000 customization in legacy  SAP ERP application
  • Manage ERP version upgrade cycle.

Value Driven Results

  • Adopting fit to standard approach has allowed Hitachi to reduce core system customization from more than 9000 to 22.
  • Developments are now reused across the landscape.
  • A drastically less customized landscape has seen update cycle reduced from every five years to yearly.

The impact

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

Business Transformation and clean core

 

How each dimension of Cloud ALM supports clean core

 

How project management can be used for clean core.

  • As clean core is a journey, it’s an ongoing process and cannot be adapted at once.
  • We need to make sure we are following the Best practices and adapting the SAP Activate methodologies to be agile compliant in our clean core journey.
  • Define teams and roles.
  • Define Timeboxes and releases.
  • Adapt Q-Gates Management
  • Overall dashboarding for tracking the project progress.

You can find the E2E project management details in my blog which was demonstrated in the webinar. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/all-the-buzz-about-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/13881779

Rise with SAP Methodology Dashboard

  • It fetches the data from multiple sources, Signavio, leanIx, Q-gates , readiness report etc.
  • Supports in making decisions which steps to be taken in what sequence for clean core adaption
  • Bridging the gap from analysis to execution by task creation and distribution among project team

Session 2 - Efficiently Managing RISE with SAP Projects: A Clean Core Approach Using SAP Cloud ALM-21st November 2024

What is Clean Core Functionality?

  • Contains tasks to learn more about clean core methodology
  • Clean core success plan is a subset of 118 tasks currently
  • Cleanup unused code
  • To be as close to standard

What is a Q-gate?

  • Predefined milestones where a project is audited to check, if it meets the necessary criteria move to next phase.
  • Checkpoints for the current defined project task

 What is the importance of Q-gates in clean core adaption?

To tack cross project clean core activities.

Automatically generated Q-gates in clean core adaption

 

Project Setup and roadmap selection for clean core adaption

There are three ways to manage clean core tasks

  • As a part of implementation project
  • As a part of system conversion
  • In a consolidation project

For all the variants Q-gates are automatically created as per the selected roadmap. Selecting a roadmap while creating a project, below are the available roadmaps in Cloud ALM for project setup.

 

Select a roadmap and SAVE, the Q-gates gets auto populated as below.

 

 

Click on the generated Q-gate to open and start working on the pre-defined checklist with available drop down options as below.

 

What is the clean core governance preparation process?

  • Create Tag group and tags

 

Below Tags were created under the tag group for clean core adaption as these are the Cloud ALM entities to establish clean core governance.

  • Process
  • Requirements
  • User Stories
  • Features

 

What is Solution Process Traceability and how it can be leveraged for clean core adaption?

To analyze the readiness of scoped solution processes based on the progress of related requirements and Test execution. We can find the answers to questions about the readiness of related entities. 

The Solution Process Traceability has been upgraded with a feature to do the indirect assignments as below:

New and planned offerings

  • To have an automated dashboard to visualize clean core related information. The dashboard will automatically populate the clean core dashboard based on the clean core related information from the below data providers:
  • Q-Gates
  • Solution Process
  • Requirements
  • User Stories
  • Features

Session 3 - Streamlining Business Processes While Preserving a Clean Core- 26th November 2024

How to transform business from strategy to execution?

Going from an ECC system to legacy S/4 Hana it's a continuous cycle, do a fit to standard workshop, document the findings and decided to redesign the process that needs to be implemented.

It’s a continuous process using SAP integrated tools.

  • SAP Signavio: SAP Signavio Process tools are primarily used for process analysis and process mining activities.
  • SAP Cloud ALM : It helps customers to implement and operate their cloud or hybrid solutions.
  • SAP Leanix: SAP LeanIX is a SaaS application that allows to manage and optimize enterprise architecture.
  • SAP Enable Now: SAP Enable Now is a digital adoption platform (DAP) from SAP that provides in-application help and e-learning content to improve the adoption, productivity, and efficiency of SAP software
  • Tricentis: Accelerates testing of all your applications, data, and business processes and integrates with tools across your delivery pipeline.

What is SAP Rise with SAP Methodology Framework?

  1. SAP Signavio is the starting point for modelling the business process.
  2. Landscape discovery involves documenting the organization's IT landscape, planning, designing, and overseeing the implementation of architectural changes to meet changing business needs.
  3. Create a project on Cloud ALM with required roadmap selection
  4. Conduct fit-to-standard workshops, to identify the gaps.
  5. Project execution begins in the realize phase with SAP Activate methodology.

 

E2E Process flow of SAP Rise with SAP Methodology Framework

 

Insights of the Clean core adaption for Business Process Transformation

  • Define Solution process and create requirements with user story and subtasks.
  • Adapt best practice solution process or customize if needed.
  • Copy solution process flow diagram into a custom process
  • Adapt Business Process accordingly.

 

Session 4 - Ensuring a Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM: Test, Change, and Deployment Management- 28th November 2024

Cloud ALM Test management capabilities

  • Test management in cloud alm can be manual or automatic through Tricentis. Test automation API’s provide liberty to integrate further 3rd party tools.
  • Test case is associated to Solution Process scope & mapped to a requirement for test Planning.
  • The test case can be built and executed manually or automatic through Tricentis.
  • A Defect can be raised through cloud alm defect Management to identify the gaps in the test results.

How  Tricentis is used to build and execute the test cases?

  • Module based test automation in the cloud.
  • Use XScan to scan screen and relevant UI elements
  • Use Builder to assemble the Test Case based Scanned Modules, Reusable test steps and SAP standard module.

 

 

Analyze the test results in a central entry for all test related information and activities in the dashboard.

 

Current change management capability of cloud alm

What is a Feature?

  • Functionality that is represented by a feature must be deployed to a target system together
  • Create a feature and assign a transport to be deployed in target systems.

 

 

  • To restrict the manual TR creation and release on development system, as per the SAP recommendation it should be done through authorizations on the managed system.
  • Mass deployment of transports to quality at the moment is only possible through the STMS import job. The deployment scheduler is aimed to provide the mass deployment functionality which will be based on release and feature status as optional where we can select transports based on a deployment plan and the target system for the deployment.
  • SAP doesn’t have any plans for custom statuses in change management, but workflow API is planned for Q1 2025, where we will be able to trigger an external workflow. SAP is also looking into providing a custom field in future so that at least this sub-status or this sub-workflow status can be shown in Cloud ALM in the feature. But it is not planned to change the main status screen of the feature to add some custom status.
  • To map a four-tier landscape in Cloud ALM SAP recommends looking into the workflow APIs as soon as they are available. For now, we can use the successfully tested status to do the checks in the QA and with the deployment scheduler feature we can have option to deploy the changes to the pre-production system. And then we can use the same approval as well for the production, so the ready for production status to render approval on the pre-prod and production level.

Current Workflow of change management in Cloud ALM

 

*Use Clean core tags to make the feature for clean core adaption.

Session 5 - Operational Excellence with SAP Cloud ALM: Driving the Clean Core Initiative – 3rd December 2024

How SAP Cloud ALM supports clean core approach adoption in Operations with its 5 principles

  • Processes -Business Process Monitoring and Job and Automation Monitoring processes are running, and we need to monitor processes and several KPIs which are actually monitored by the business process monitoring application.
  • Extensibility -   To drive business continuity of extensions for the Clean Core adoption is that when we try to do modifications and extensions in S4 system directly. SAP recommends not to modify too much as makes it easier to upgrade the systems.
  • Data – The data is exchanged between different Cloud solutions, if we consider master data, we need to ensure that this data is exchanged between the process and is running as expected. The integration monitoring application is developed to start with message-based monitoring more at technical level, and we cover events as data exchange at different technologies. SAP is now trying to bring a kind of a business object view in these exchange processes to support the data dimension of clean core.
  • Integration - Integration and exception monitoring is an entire application dedicated to the monitoring and supporting of   all type of integration artifacts from S/4 HANA, CPI, master data integration. We need to make sure that integration and data exchange processes are monitored and is happening in a reliable way. This helps in visualizing the message exchange between the systems with the status of the messages, so that we can understand if there is a problem in the integration between your systems to keep the integration clean
  •  Operations – To have a holistic and secure application and business process operations, SAP is planning to have operations as to help the customers understand how the landscape is running and how it can be optimized to have a clean system.

Clean Core Operation Dashboards Overview

There are multiple dashboards planned to give a deep dive the possibility to evaluate the quality of operations and take measures accordingly to optimize the solution for a clean core adaption.

 Operations Dashboards preview for Clean Core project

 

 

New & Planned offerings on the Cloud ALM Roadmap for clean core adoption

 

Planned Future Roadmap to enhance Cloud ALM capability

 

Link to Roadmap explorer : SAP Road Map Explorer

Key takeaways: ​SAP Cloud ALM for clean core adaption

  • A clean core helps customers to stay current and agile compliant with innovation from SAP
  • Clean core is a journey, which needs to be adapted by following a step-by-step approach
  • SAP Cloud ALM provides contribution for all five dimensions (processes, extensibility, data, integration, operations) of SAP’s Clean Core initiative.
  • Customers can build custom processes and functionality using side-by-side or built in extensions
  • Change Management in Cloud ALM is planned to be enhanced by Q4 2024 / Q1 2025
  • End to end monitoring drives business continuity and quality of process execution
  • SAP Cloud ALM operations will become the platform for clean core dashboards.
  •  SAP plans to provide operation status as well as improvement dashboards covering business service availability, system operations, application operations as well as business process operations.
  • SAP plans to provide security status as well as improvement dashboards covering security configuration as well as security runtime aspects.

Thank you for reading the blog, I hope this helps you find a summary on Clean Core Webinars.

Note: Some pictures have been taken from the slides shared by SAP for the webinar series.

entry.title: Re: Today is ROCKY DAY in the city of brotherly love! (What is your favorite Rocky movie??)
entry.content: Yo, Rocky!

entry.title: Re: Can dimensions be rearranged in a SAC model?
entry.content: Thanks Soniya for your response. However, I am referring to rearranging the dimensions in the "Model Structure" and not within a story.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI
entry.content: @Karthikeshwaran you could check for APIs, but no options that I'm aware of

entry.title: Re: Error-assigning a company code to a controlling area
entry.content:
Check the ledger definition, as well as the company code and the control area in Customizing.

  • This is a general error (incorrect or inconsistent settings, unable to find information on ledger) and the relevant information is not displayed in your post.

entry.title: Re: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Hello @yogananda, 

Thank you very much for your support!!! I will study the links.

My question is, for example, if I map the user like this (Default Code): 

 { "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName", 
"targetPath": "$.loginName", 
"correlationAttribute": true }

Then, the userPrincipalName (email address) in Azure will be mapped to loginName (which is also created as an email address, as it’s provisioned from Azure) in IAS.

When I provision the user to SAC, the username in SAC is created as an email address, which SAC doesn't allow ->an error occurs.

How can I ensure that the userPrincipalName is not passed as an email address to the cloud application?

 

Thank you very much.

Best Regards

entry.title: Is the side-by-side BTP training updated with new course content? Found some bugs
entry.content:
Looks like this course contents are updated with different business example.

from risk-management -> incident-management. Unfortunately, we cannot copy code to test by ourselves and demo exercises are not clearly defined. There are few more bugs in the new content.

How to copy code?

Is it announced anywhere that SAP BTP is going to update the course content?

Please guide on this. Thanks.

Ref: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/build-side-by-side-extensions-on-sap-btp

entry.title: Re: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content: @tskwin you will have to bring user from Entra to IAS and IAS to other SAP applications. once you bring users to IAS with populated details to respective fields.. you can map it properly to target systems according. you can refer my blog how you can map from source to target with fields

entry.title: Re: Migrate trust configuration from SAML to OIDC in BTP subaccount
entry.content:
Hi Ani,

Thanks for sharing the helpful info. Could you also provide some more info about the below.

If we change from SAML to OIDC protocol will it have any effect with XSUAA being used in BTP apps developed.

entry.title: Re: CAP Application for Business Process Automation approval form?
entry.content: Hello Adam, I hope we resolved this issue, or Are you still encountering an issue ?

entry.title: Re: SQL Anywhere 17 Read-only scale-out: how does the roo...
entry.content: That's basically what I wanted to know. Thanks.

entry.title: Is there any base for this profit center check in production orders
entry.content:
While creating a production order without material a profit center check gets triggered and a warning message is showing.

But in ECC this error is not showing. Got some note 2723379, 2975320 but not getting the base for this check.

Any idea?

entry.title: Need information on migrating from SAML protocol to OIDC
entry.content:
Hi,

Could anyone provide the information on the below that would be helpful.

We are planning to migrate the Trust configuration between the SAP BTP account and Cloud Identity services from SAML to OIDC.

If we migrate does the new change will have effect on the current XSUAA service being utilized in developing the SAP BTP UI5 and CAPM Apps.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Build Toggle one Item on screen does not work and Workflow activation
entry.content: Hello Boris, Thanks for reaching out to us, can you please share the exact blog here for further investigation ?

entry.title: Re: Employee as contact person
entry.content:
Have you tried this? adding it as a contact in relationships. 

In the account it appears under relationships

Though this is not exactly what you are looking for (since its a personal contact and appears under relationships and not a contact person under the contacts tab), wondering if this could work.

Julius

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code Storyboard
entry.content: Hi @T1mey Sorry about the inconvenience, the latest release (including the application logic editor) was rolled back due to a compatible issue, it's assumed to be recovered soon. I can keep you updated once it's ready.

entry.title: Re: Disabling the Item Selection Check-boxes of List Outp...
entry.content:
open the page map and do the customization for that table

"tableSettings": {
"selectionMode": "None",

entry.title: Re: Rejection/ Reminder for RFQ's via Fiori using BRF+ ou...
entry.content: Thank you for your reply

entry.title: Re: Conversion of Json data to xml in build process autom...
entry.content:
Hello Santosh,

Thanks for reaching out to us, please find the help portal for Json2xml SDK  https://help.sap.com/docs/intelligent-robotic-process-automation/desktop-sdk-reference-guide/json2xml?locale=en-US&q=JSON+to+XML

Please let us know in case of you need further clarification, provide with more details in screenshot of error.

entry.title: That one is for you, SAP ARIBA Procurement professional ! Let´s prepare for the future.
entry.content:
A new blog post by Etosha (Chief Marketing Office) is now live on the SAP News Center: Bet Big on Yourself in 2025. In it, she highlights five key “bets” spend management professionals should make to ensure the success and agility of their organizations in the face of economic and political uncertainty.

Bet #1: Strategic Collaboration

Bet #2: Effortless Compliance

Bet #3: Seamless Integration

Bet #4: Increased Productivity

Bet #5: Informed Decision-Making

Drawing from the recent SAP Spend Connect Live and the 2024 Economist Impact report, Etosha underscores the growing importance of the procurement function and how professionals can prepare for future challenges. She emphasizes how SAP’s solutions support each of these areas, providing a platform for collaboration, simplifying compliance, offering seamless integration, boosting productivity, and enabling data-driven decisions.

(NOTE ; If your Browser allows, just click with the right button on the text to get imediate translation !)

ENJOY !!!

entry.title: Error in ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 HDB
entry.content: Hi, when I start the docker desktop for A4H (/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01), I see error in HDB. Refer to below screenshot. Need help to fix this error. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Material Quantity Calculation result is not copying based on batch determination
entry.content: Hello, I have the same problem, did you manage to solve it?

entry.title: Re: Admin Proxy in the New Learning Experience
entry.content: When Learning admins proxy users, they're to see the legacy Learning user interface, not the new one. That's the current system behavior (KBA 3511497). The fix that was mentioned in the KBA 3531315 has been already delivered, the problem doesn't occur anymore. The problem was: "When Learning admins launch proxy from the User entity ("Action" menu), the new Learning user interface displays instead of the legacy one".

 Alexander

entry.title: Re: Partner function in equipment.
entry.content:
I believe you need to Create and Assign Partner Determination Procedure to Equipment Category. Partner Determination for Plant Maintenance is generally maintained (in on Premise) under Master Data in Plant Maintenance and Customer Service - Basic Settings - Partners. 

May be your Plant Maintenance IT Team can help you here.

entry.title: Re: Today is ROCKY DAY in the city of brotherly love! (What is your favorite Rocky movie??)
entry.content: @JimSpath that is a better idea - what is your favorite Rocky line?😁

entry.title: Re: Step By Step Embedded SLT Configuration & Realtime Replication to SAP BTP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, thanks for sharing this!

I have one question, does this work for SLT from ECC system as well or only from S/4HANA?

entry.title: Outstanding Feedback Reporting
entry.content:
Hi all is it possible to report on who has been sent a feedback request however has not replied to it?  We havent got Story reports switched on as yet

the Business asked me for a report to identify feedback requests that had not been responded to. As far as I can see, the feedback records only become available for reporting once the feedback response has been submitted. I can't find any way to report feedback requests that haven't been responded to yet. Do you know if I'm missing something? Thank  you

entry.title: Re: Error-assigning a company code to a controlling area
entry.content: Thank you but mu issue is an error pops up when I assign the two objects

entry.title: Re: How to trigger IDOC based on status change on Materia...
entry.content:
Just a brain-dump: You could set up a DRF (Data Replication Framework) replication based on communication scenario SAP_COM_0009.

In the filter definition (separate step from creating the communication arrangement), you can then define filter to only include business objects with "cross-plant material status" 71 or 72.

This would then trigger replication whenever a product with these status is created/changed (so not only when the status 71/72 is first set).

entry.title: Re: Today is ROCKY DAY in the city of brotherly love! (What is your favorite Rocky movie??)
entry.content: I wasn't able to get to the famous Rocky steps in the city! I watched a really good interview with Sylvester Stallone. He grew up in Philadelphia, which I didn't realize!!

entry.title: Re: Business Partner and Product Master Data Replication from On Premise to BTP using DRF
entry.content:
@Omar-Ghatrify

You can develop an IFlow in CPI with multiple receiversystems. You only need to configure one logical port in SOAMANAGER that points to the URL of the IFlow.

If you want to keep each IFlow separately, you would need to create a corresponding port in SOAMANAGER for each IFlow.

entry.title: Re: SAP Approuter Headers using Private Link Service for Azure Blob Storage
entry.content: Thanks for your response. The reason to use an Approuter is, that as far as i know SAP Cloud Integration (resp. Integration Suite) does not support Private Link Services directly. The App router acts as a proxy as described here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-private-link-service-use-cases-for-sap-cloud-integration-and-sap-build/ba-p/13542464 (see section "How to achieve this"). As this blog post is from 2 years ago, this might be outdated but I am not aware of a way to connect PLS with Integration Suite directly. In my opinion this is not an authentication issue, but rather a missing feature of the approuter.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign the iRPA road to SAP Build Process Automation

Kind regards

Jana

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@GovindaRaoBanothu 

@SAPHCM2 

welcome! May I asked where you are based? I am in the US outside of Philadelphia.

Be sure to 'join' the interest groups and locations groups to stay connected.

Do you have ideas of what you want to take advantage of in the community to increase your knowledge and to network with other members?
Best of luck!

entry.title: Do you know the different extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Enhancing Key User & Side-by-Side Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview 
This LJ covers two important extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA that are available to customers and partners:

  • Key-User extensibility focuses on adaptations created by key users that have a deep business knowledge. Key-User extensibility comprises adding new fields or custom logic, adapting the standard UIs, creating custom UIs, and creating custom business objects.
  • Side-by-side extensibility on SAP Business Technology Platform allows developers to build their own applications, enhancing and supplementing existing SAP S/4HANA (on premise) apps and SAP S/4HANA Cloud apps.

Learning objectives

  • Get to know about the extensibility concept in SAP S/4HANA
  • Personalize the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Manage the SAP Fiori launchpad content
  • Extend the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Use variant management
  • Use SAP Fiori runtime authoring
  • Create data source extensions
  • Create custom fields
  • Enable custom database fields for usage in SAP Fiori applications
  • Create custom logic
  • Create custom business objects
  • Create a UI for a custom business object
  • Add custom logic to a custom business object
  • Create custom libraries and methods
  • Create custom code lists
  • Transport extension items
  • Describe essential features of side-by-side extensions
  • Name the most important factors that influence the choice of the extensibility option
  • Develop an extension for SAP S/4HANA with SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Prerequisites

  • Introduction to Application Programming on SAP 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margi
